commit.message:
Add comprehensive scaling procedures for all deployment components

The agent lacked clear guidance on how to handle scaling requests.
This adds scaling examples to the agent description, expands the
SKILL.md scaling section with per-component summaries, documents the
worker host list update requirement, and adds a full database scaling
procedure (regenerate Postgres cmd via generate_pg_cmd.py). Also
covers other accessories with memory-dependent configuration.

- PATH B (scan-product fallback) blijft intact als safety net

commit.message:
feat: add field topology analysis with phase detection and axis dominance

Computes the overall "shape" of the semantic field:
- Total/mean/max scores, active cell count
- Entropy and concentration metrics (how focused vs diffuse the field is)
- Phase classification: dormant / focused / clustered / transitional / diffuse
- Dominant axis detection per dimension (WHAT/WHERE/TIME)
- Per-layer energy distribution (floor/middle/top)
- Variance and standard deviation of score distribution

Integrated into emergence snapshot, API endpoints, and ASCII renderer.
63 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix LF/HF computation and add scipy dependency

- Replace wall-clock timestamps with cumulative RR-interval time axis;
  multiple RR intervals per BLE packet share the same timestamp, so
  np.interp was producing garbage interpolation
- Upgrade detrend from mean-subtraction to scipy linear detrend for
  better spectral estimation
- Add scipy>=1.11.0 to requirements.txt (imported by stats.py but
  previously missing, causing ImportError on fresh install)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit — knobly-wobly component library

Audio UI components for React. Headless hooks, themed components,
and a parameter model designed for music production interfaces.

- Knob component with 270° SVG arc, thumb indicator, drag/scroll/keyboard
- Toggle and Button components
- Zustand-based parameter store with curves and formatting
- CSS custom property token system (dark theme)
- Next.js docs site with live demos

SymbolDetective: check IMAN_classification on parent Item, not just revision

Classification in TC is often applied at Item level (PLM4Symbol) rather
than ItemRevision level. Now explicitly loads IMAN_classification on the
parent item and folds any ICOs found into the classification lookup.
Console message distinguishes which level the classification was found at.

commit.message:
feat: add "Use tools without approval" setting + bump v2026.3.4

Add OpenClaw section in Settings with a toggle to control tools.exec
security config in openclaw.json. When enabled (default), sets
security:"full" and ask:"off" so agents run tools freely. When
disabled, sets security:"allowlist" and ask:"on-miss".

Config is enforced at app launch, gateway start, and on toggle change
(with automatic gateway restart). Overrides OpenClaw's restrictive
defaults on first install.

feat(task-385): 12-Asset Holy Grail Portfolio + Feature API Hardening

Phase 1 — Portfolio Matrix 重构:
- 停用 ETHUSD/SOLUSD/XRPUSD (BTC 相关性 ~0.90 高同质化)
- 新增 9 品种: USDJPY/XAUUSD/XAGUSD/BRENT/XNGUSD/US100/US500/HK50/DE40
- global_confidence_threshold=0.55 (6大板块龙头基准 Edge)
- TrackType 扩容: 12活跃 + 3遗留 = 15条目
- live_strategies.yaml v3.0.0: 12策略全激活 + max_daily_loss_pct=50
- assets.yaml: mt5_eodhd_symbol_map 完整12+3品种

Phase 2 — Feature API Hardening (Tri-Brain 驱动):
- Pydantic V2 LatestFeatureRequest/HistoricalFeatureRequest 替代 raw dict
- Optional API Key 认证 (FEATURE_API_KEY env-var gate)
- iterrows 反模式 → 向量化 (bb_position/macd_strength/sma_trend/volatility_ratio)
- compute_features() 全量 _safe_float() NaN 保护
- HISTORICAL_BAR_BUFFER = 250 常量化
- API Key 脱敏日志 ([:4]+'***'+[-4:])
- _safe_float(v: Any) 类型提示

Tests: 15/15 PASS | Tri-Brain: audit_pass x2 | MASTER_ARCHIVE: v2.99.9 Section12.75 | CC: v16.28.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(relationships): switch to hard delete + cascade reciprocal

Replace soft delete (status: 'terminated') with hard delete (actual
Firestore removal). Soft delete caused relationships to reappear after
save because of stale cache and PendingRelationshipGuard auto-submit.

Also cascade-deletes reciprocal relationships (e.g., client↔vendor,
partner↔partner) to prevent orphaned reverse entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: layout overhaul — hide controls during reading, maximize wheel visibility

- Revert wheel compact/shrink approach — wheel stays full size (95vw/550px)
- ReadingDim now fully hides controls (return null) instead of dimming
- Hide view toggle, day nav, and birth chart CTA during reading
- Header compact mode: hide subtitle and date/location line during reading
- Zero padding on wheel container when reading active (pt-0 pb-0)
- Add overflow-x-hidden to page root to prevent horizontal scroll
- Wheel gets all space from header to reading card bottom sheet

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Configure localhost SMTP defaults for Plesk and add email docs

- Default SMTP to localhost:25 (works on Plesk without auth)
- Transport creation triggers on MILESTONE_NOTIFY_EMAIL being set
- Add tls.rejectUnauthorized=false for localhost connections
- Document email configuration in DEPLOYMENT.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add working gripper with full parallel-jaw linkage and protocol support

Wire the xArm gripper end-to-end through the Viam app: Open/Grab test
cards now drive the simulated gripper via the xArm Modbus TCP protocol.

Key changes:
- Emulator: invert gripper direction mapping (protocol 0=closed/850=open
  vs Gazebo 0=open/0.85=closed), return target position for instant
  convergence in the module's polling loop
- Gripper drive_joint: switch from PID force mode to velocity command
  mode (DART FORCE actuator cannot move this joint)
- gripper_mimic.py: Gazebo Python system plugin that copies drive_joint
  position to all 5 linkage joints each physics step, bypassing DART's
  inability to actuate secondary gripper joints via controllers
- All gripper joints converted from fixed to revolute with correct mimic
  multipliers (+1/-1) from xarm_description URDF for symmetric closure
  and parallel fingertips
- Table model consolidated to single link (compatible with both DART and
  bullet-featherstone physics engines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve auth persistence and fix mobile Google sign-in

Switch Firebase persistence from localStorage-only to IndexedDB-first
(with localStorage fallback) for more durable sessions. Use
signInWithRedirect on mobile devices instead of signInWithPopup, which
is unreliable on mobile browsers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Convert Text.kt comments from // / to KDoc

Mechanical conversion of all JavaScript-style // / comments to
proper /** */ KDoc on Text, TextIterator, and Line classes.
Fixes Dokka output and IDE quick-documentation for the core
document data structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix NaN in projections.json breaking JSON.parse in browser

Python's json.dump(allow_nan=True) was writing NaN values for
injury_insight fields. JavaScript's JSON.parse() rejects NaN as
invalid JSON, causing the entire projections load to fail silently.
This is why the dashboard showed 0 player props despite data existing.

Fixed 9 NaN in projections.json and 190 in all_props.json.

commit.message:
Unify atom-to-type-ref resolution between spectra and spectra_openapi (#120)

* Unify atom-to-type-ref resolution between spectra and spectra_openapi

Extract normalize_type_ref/2 into spectra_util so both spectra.erl and
spectra_openapi.erl use the same logic to resolve a plain atom (e.g. `user`)
into a type reference ({type, user, 0} or {record, user}). Previously
spectra_openapi required explicit {type, t, 0} tuples while spectra.erl
accepted plain atoms.

* Auto-populate description from type doc in parameters, request bodies, and response headers

When a parameter, request body, or response header references a type that has
a spectra doc with a description, the description is now automatically used
without needing to pass it explicitly. Explicit descriptions still override
the type doc. deprecated is also propagated for parameters and headers.

commit.message:
Paper 3: Scaling of Perspectival Curvature (N=8 to N=20)

Four experiments at N=20 (Hilbert dim 2^20 = 1,048,576) via sparse Lanczos:
1. Partiality-variance: σ drops 34x from k/N=0.20→1.0, mean R≈0.24 constant
2. Entanglement weakening: FRC-entropy r drops 0.94→0.48
3. Topology: chain more negative than all-to-all (median gap ~-0.05, p<0.01
   Wilcoxon, 200 trials per k). Does not increase with partiality.
4. Perspective convergence: σ narrows with N (0.81→0.09) but persists

Includes min_distance parameter on ORC computation (curvature.py),
200-trial extended topology sweep, and all reproduction scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui): resolve keep-alive 404s and /auth/me route mismatch

Crow's CROW_CATCHALL_ROUTE double-called res.end() on keep-alive
connections — the CatchallRule wrapper called it, then Router::handle
called it again. The second end() left completed_=true as stale state,
causing every other request on the same connection to short-circuit
to a 404 without invoking the handler.

Switch to (request, response&) void signature so the wrapper stores
the lambda directly without auto-calling res.end(), letting
Router::handle be the sole caller.

Also fix auth.ts me() calling /api/v1/me instead of /api/v1/auth/me.

commit.message:
fix: enable GT911 touch via manual GPIO 1 reset before lcd.begin()

The GT911 capacitive touch controller on the CrowPanel Advance 5.0"
(V1.1 board) requires a 120ms reset pulse on GPIO 1 before it appears
on the I2C bus. Without this, the GT911 is absent at address 0x5D and
all touch input fails silently.

LovyanGFX's Touch_GT911::init() has its own reset sequence, but it
only holds RST low for 1ms — far too short for the GT911 which needs
100-200ms post-reset initialization time. Setting PIN_TOUCH_RST=-1
prevents LovyanGFX from doing its own inadequate reset, while our
manual 120ms reset + 300ms recovery ensures the GT911 is fully
initialized before Touch_GT911::init() attempts I2C communication.

Verified: I2C scan shows GT911 at 0x5D after reset, and product ID
reads "911 " confirming successful I2C communication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Merge development into main (#447)

* test: document activeElement guard difference between checked and value in SetAttr (#427)

The `value` branch in SetAttr skips updating el.value when the element is
document.activeElement (to protect in-progress user typing). The `checked`
branch intentionally has no such guard because checkboxes have no "typing"
state — the server is sole source of truth via dj-click preventDefault.

Adds two regression tests documenting this intentional behavioral difference:
1. SetAttr checked updates el.checked even when checkbox is activeElement
2. SetAttr value skips el.value update when text input is activeElement

Closes #425

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add RemoveAttr checked test for focused checkbox (activeElement) (#429)

Complete the coverage matrix for checked property sync on focused elements.
The existing tests covered SetAttr checked on a focused checkbox and
RemoveAttr checked on an unfocused element, but not RemoveAttr checked
when the checkbox is document.activeElement.

This confirms the intentional design: unlike the value branch (which skips
focused elements to protect in-progress typing), the checked branch always
applies server-authoritative state regardless of focus.

Closes #428

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: improve getting-started guide, VDOM explanation, and doc cross-links (#430)

- Add "How Reactivity Works" section to README with dj-root, dj-view,
  dj-click attribute explanations and one-sided {% if %} pitfall
- Add step-by-step "Getting Started" walkthrough (5 steps, zero to counter)
- Add "Next steps" links to template cheatsheet, components, CSS frameworks,
  and deployment guides
- Fix urlize|safe anti-pattern in README code examples
- Fix broken CSS framework guide link (docs/guides → docs/website/guides)
- Add 11 new doc-completeness test assertions (39 total) covering README
  cross-links, components guide, and deployment guide content
- Update template cheatsheet with pitfall section and keyed list docs
- Expand VDOM architecture doc with pitfall and form-value sections
- Add CSS frameworks guide (Tailwind/Bootstrap setup and CI/CD)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: TypeScript definitions and Python type stubs for public API (#158) (#434)

* feat: rename data-dj-id to dj-id, remove implicit id= key fallback, add dj-key

Breaking changes for v1.0:

1. Rename VDOM tracking attribute data-dj-id → dj-id for consistency with
   all other dj- prefixed attributes (dj-view, dj-click, dj-model, etc.).
   - parser.rs stamps dj-id instead of data-dj-id
   - diff.rs syncs and ignores dj-id (not data-dj-id) when diffing attrs
   - All querySelector calls updated: [data-dj-id=] → [dj-id=]
   - System check T011 warns when templates contain data-dj-id attributes

2. Remove implicit id= fallback for keyed VDOM diffing. Developers using
   id= on elements will no longer have those silently used as diff keys.
   - dj-key and data-key both work as explicit opt-in keyed diffing
   - parser.rs: removed id_attr extraction and .or(id_attr) fallback
   - Tests updated: id-as-key tests now assert key == None

Also:
- Add docs/guides/lists.md with dj-key keyed diffing guide
- Update MIGRATION.md with both breaking changes and migration checklist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add SSE fallback transport for corporate proxy environments

When WebSocket connections are blocked by corporate proxies or enterprise
firewalls, djust now automatically falls back to Server-Sent Events (SSE).

Architecture:
- Server→Client: EventSource (GET /djust/sse/<session_id>/)
- Client→Server: HTTP POST (/djust/sse/<session_id>/event/)
- Transport negotiation is automatic: WS tried first, SSE on failure

New files:
- python/djust/sse.py: DjustSSEStreamView, DjustSSEEventView, SSESession,
  shared mount/event dispatch helpers, sse_urlpatterns
- python/djust/static/djust/src/03b-sse.js: LiveViewSSE class with same
  interface as LiveViewWebSocket for transparent transport switching
- docs/sse-transport.md: setup guide, feature limitations table, security notes
- python/tests/test_sse.py: 20 Python tests (all passing)
- tests/js/sse.test.js: 17 JS tests (all passing)

Modified files:
- 03-websocket.js: add onTransportFailed callback hook (fires instead of
  showing error overlay when SSE transport is available)
- 14-init.js: _switchToSSETransport() helper + SSE fallback wiring in djustInit
- demo_project/urls.py: register sse_urlpatterns under /djust/
- CHANGELOG.md, README.md: document feature, mark roadmap item complete

Feature limitations vs WebSocket (documented in docs/sse-transport.md):
no binary uploads, no presence tracking, no actors, no MessagePack

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add TypeScript definitions and Python type stubs for public API

Adds `djust.d.ts` — a comprehensive ambient TypeScript declaration file
shipped with the Python package at `static/djust/djust.d.ts`. Covers
the full `window.djust` public API surface:

- `DjustHook` lifecycle interface (mounted, beforeUpdate, updated,
  destroyed, disconnected, reconnected)
- `DjustHookContext` (this.el, this.viewName, this.pushEvent,
  this.handleEvent)
- `LiveViewWebSocket` and `LiveViewSSE` transport classes
- Navigation helpers (DjustNavigation, handleNavigation, resolveViewPath)
- dj-model binding (bindModelElements)
- Streaming API (DjustStreamMessage, DjustStreamOp, DjustStreamInfo)
- Upload progress types (DjustUploadEntry, DjustUploadConfig,
  DjustUploadProgressEventDetail, djust:upload:progress custom event)
- `declare global` Window extension with djust, DjustHooks, djustDebug

Also adds `python/djust/_rust.pyi` — PEP 561 type stubs for the PyO3
Rust extension module, enabling IDE autocomplete and mypy type checking
for all Rust-injected functions and classes.

Includes 27 tests in `python/tests/test_typescript_definitions.py`
validating file structure, interface completeness, nullable types,
optional parameters, and balanced braces.

README roadmap and CONTRIBUTING updated to reflect completion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add djust-theming standalone PyPI package (#158)

Extracts theming utilities from the demo project into a standalone
`packages/djust-theming/` sub-package publishable to PyPI as
`djust-theming==0.3.0`.

- Pure-Python Django app with no dependency on djust core
- Bundles theme.css (design tokens, dark/light mode), components.css,
  utilities.css, and theme-switcher.js as Django static files
- Install via `pip install djust-theming`, add `djust_theming` to
  INSTALLED_APPS, then use `{% static 'djust_theming/css/theme.css' %}`

  with trusted publishing to PyPI (environment: pypi-djust-theming)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: include dj-if placeholder comments in getSignificantChildren (#369)

Without this fix, RemoveChild patches targeting <!--dj-if--> comment
nodes (emitted by baa3ca8) fail because getSignificantChildren filtered
ALL comments. The fix makes dj-if comments significant children so
patch indices align with the Rust VDOM.

Also updates the inline filter in getNodeByPath for consistency.

Closes #369

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: support Jinja2-style inline conditionals in {{ }} expressions

Adds support for `{{ true_expr if condition else false_expr }}` syntax
inside `{{ }}` variable expressions. This is safe to use inside HTML
attribute values, unlike `{% if %}` blocks which insert `<!--dj-if-->`
comment nodes that corrupt attribute strings.

Examples:
  class="{{ 'btn--active' if view_mode == 'day' else '' }}"
  disabled="{{ 'disabled' if is_locked else '' }}"
  class="{{ 'error' if has_error }}"   {# else is optional #}

Implementation:
- New Node::InlineIf { true_expr, condition, false_expr, filters }
- Parser detects ` if ` keyword (skipping quoted strings) in {{ }} content
- Renderer evaluates condition via existing evaluate_condition(), then
  calls get_value() for the chosen branch — no DOM nodes emitted
- inheritance.rs: node_to_template_string handles InlineIf round-trip
- 3 new parser tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle #comment vnode in createNodeFromVNode

When the VDOM diff sends an InsertChild patch for a <!--dj-if-->
placeholder comment node, createNodeFromVNode received tag='#comment'
but only handled '#text' — falling through to the tag whitelist,
failing, and creating a <span> placeholder instead. This caused
'[LiveView] Blocked unknown tag, using span placeholder' warnings and
incorrect DOM structure.

Fix: add explicit #comment case before the tag whitelist check, using
document.createComment(vnode.text).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update djust.d.ts streaming types to match JS implementation

Add "done" and "start" stream ops, "mode" field for text ops, and fix
getActiveStreams() return type from Map to Record to match the actual
JavaScript implementation in 17-streaming.js.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add --server flag and server_url to deploy CLI credentials (#437)

- Add --server CLI flag (with DJUST_SERVER env var) to override default server URL
- Store server_url in credentials JSON alongside token and email
- Use Click context to propagate server URL to all subcommands
- Add tests for --server flag, env var override, and credential overwrite
- Add CHANGELOG entry for djust-deploy CLI

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert Cargo.lock crate versions from 0.3.3-rc.2 to 0.3.5 (#439)

The development branch had a stale Cargo.lock that downgraded all five
crates (djust_components, djust_core, djust_live, djust_templates,
djust_vdom) from 0.3.5 to 0.3.3-rc.2. This was unrelated to any feature
work — likely committed from a developer's working tree with old lockfile
state. Restored Cargo.lock from main to match the authoritative versions.

Closes #431

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add security comment to custom adapter example in docs (#440)

The custom adapter example in css-frameworks.md sets required_marker to
raw HTML that is rendered without escaping (by design in frameworks.py).
Add a safety comment warning against interpolating user data to prevent
copy-paste XSS vulnerabilities.

Closes #432

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add Django migration note for urlize safe filter behavior (#441)

In standard Django, `urlize` requires `|safe` to render its HTML output.
djust's Rust template engine automatically marks `urlize`, `urlizetrunc`,
and `unordered_list` as safe via the `safe_output_filters` list in the
renderer, so `|safe` is unnecessary. Added a blockquote note after the
template syntax example in README.md to clarify this for users migrating
from Django.

Closes #433

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify urlize safe_output_filters behavior vs standard Django (#445)

* fix: revert Cargo.lock crate versions from 0.3.3-rc.2 to 0.3.5

The development branch had a stale Cargo.lock that downgraded all five
crates (djust_components, djust_core, djust_live, djust_templates,
djust_vdom) from 0.3.5 to 0.3.3-rc.2. This was unrelated to any feature
work — likely committed from a developer's working tree with old lockfile
state. Restored Cargo.lock from main to match the authoritative versions.

Closes #431

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify urlize safe_output_filters behavior vs standard Django

README line ~459 had `{{ body|urlize|safe }}` changed to
`{{ body|urlize }}`. The comment was verbose and unclear; replaced
with a brief note explaining that djust's Rust engine auto-marks
urlize output as safe via the `safe_output_filters` whitelist —
unlike standard Django where `|safe` must be added explicitly.

Closes #433

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove deprecated `default_app_config` from djust-theming (removed in

- Add CHANGELOG entry for TypeScript type stub changes (new stream ops,
  mode field, Record return type)
- Document Google Fonts CDN dependency in djust-theming README with
  self-hosted font alternative note

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove duplicate djust-theming from monorepo

djust-theming lives in its own repo (djust-org/djust-theming) and should
not be duplicated inside packages/. Also removes the publish workflow
since the standalone repo handles its own CI/CD.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Store ride images locally from Wikipedia Commons

Disney CDN hotlink blocking. Updated RIDE_IMAGES const to reference
local paths via BASE_URL so they work on both dev and GH Pages.

commit.message:
[refactor] Reduce advisory board to 2 advisors, peer review to 3 reviewers

- Advisory board: 4→2 (domain expert + journal reviewer)
  Merged methodologist + evidence synthesizer focus into domain expert
- Peer review: 4→3 (methodologist + structure editor + senior editor)
  Merged literature expert's coverage into methodologist persona
- Saves ~40% API cost per pipeline run

commit.message:
Fix ECG/Echo parser: aliases, imaging fallback, and tool-use examples

- Add alias matching in parse_action() before fuzzy matching to prevent
  "Electrocardiogram" silently misrouting to "Echocardiogram" (score 84)
  and to recognize common clinical names (EKG, TTE, TEE, Cardiac Echo, etc.)
- Add imaging->standalone tool fallback in parse_imaging_action_input()
  so "Action: Imaging / Action Input: Echocardiogram..." redirects to the
  standalone Echocardiogram tool instead of raising InvalidActionError
- Add ECG_TOOL_USE_EXAMPLE and ECHO_TOOL_USE_EXAMPLE in prompts.py and
  inject them in agent.py/tot_agent.py when cardiac_tools=True
- Skip action-input-required check when action redirected to ECG/Echo

These fixes address the 32% invalid tool rate on chest conditions reported
in README (vs 17% for abdominal), which was partly caused by parser
limitations rather than genuine model hallucination.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore school logos in trade offer rows

Show school names with logos inline on a single line per trade,
balancing readability with compact layout.
